name: Functional Reactive Programming (FRP)

corePrinciples: Composition by pure transformation, not callbacks
x.controlDataFlow: Declarative dataflow: you describe how outputs derive from time-varying inputs; the runtime propagates changes.
x.suitableProjects: UIs, animation, simulations, and anything modelling values that change continuously or discretely over time.

def: A declarative paradigm that models time-varying values and event streams as first-class, composable values transformed by pure functions.
problem: Callback-driven state updates become tangled ("callback hell") and hard to reason about as interactions grow.
mechanism: Represents inputs as continuous behaviours and discrete event streams; outputs are pure functions of them. A propagation engine recomputes dependents when sources change, ideally glitch-free.
limits: Learning curve; leaky abstractions in big graphs
Glitches / ordering issues in naive implementations
failures: Confusing FRP with general reactive (Rx) libraries
whenNot: Hard real-time where propagation cost is unpredictable
myths: "FRP = reactive programming" — classical FRP has precise continuous-time semantics; most Rx libraries are reactive but not FRP
